summary: Audio→MIDI conversion via Spotify Basic Pitch (TFJS) verified end-to-end. Model files serve correctly; WAV upload triggers conversion progress UI, produces notes, persists via POST /api/songs, auto-selects the new song, and playback works. Unsupported extensions are rejected with a toast.
passed: GET /basic-pitch-model/model.json returns 200 with valid TFJS graph-model JSON
Unsupported .txt file shows 'Unsupported file' toast and does not initiate conversion
Uploading /tmp/test_melody.wav shows conversion-progress element with stage ('DETECTING PITCHES') and percent
upload-button becomes disabled and displays 'Working' with spinner during conversion
Conversion completed within ~30s; progress bar detached after completion
POST /api/songs returned 200; new song persisted to backend
New song 'test_melody' (4s, 6 notes) appears at top of Uploaded section and is auto-selected as current
Play button starts playback (transport advances to 0:03); rolling notes area + piano keyboard render
Success toast 'Converted 6 notes from audio' displayed
Zero console errors during entire flow
notes: MIDI file upload regression path and demo song play/pause/stop/speed regressions were not explicitly re-executed in this iteration but share the same code paths (parseMidiFile + engine.play/pause/stop). Play button was clicked on the newly-converted audio-derived song and worked without errors, exercising the shared engine.
